visual_attributes.morphology: Chimeric creature with a combination of terrestrial and aquatic features
distinctive_features: Head and jaws of a crocodile
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Large, comparable to a crocodile or elephant
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Front half terrestrial, back half aquatic

domain_specific_visual.Vahana: Vehicle of river goddess Ganga and god Varuna
domain_specific_visual.Temple_iconography: Commonly depicted in Hindu and Buddhist temples

domain_specific_functional.Astrological_significance: Equivalent to Capricorn in Hindu astrology
domain_specific_functional.Ceremonial_role: Used in temple architecture and jewelry

relationships.description: Makara serves as the vahana or vehicle of the river goddess Ganga.
Makara is the vehicle of Varuna, the god of the ocean.
Makara is the emblem of Kamadeva, the god of love.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: South Asia, particularly India and Sri Lanka
